summary: Matched the common fighter `ftattacks4combo` object by replacing the hand-written assembly file with C, marking the object as Matching, and updating build lists, splits, symbols, and the header prototype. The implementation covers the side-smash combo transition helper, AttackS42 animation/IASA/physics/collision wrappers, and the Link/Young Link-style fighter-kind check needed before entering motion state 0x155. Review focused mainly on correcting the object path in `obj_files.mk` and resolving missing includes in Link/Young Link init files, though those init-file changes are only mentioned in comments and are not visible in the provided changed-file list.
what_changed: Added a C implementation in `src/melee/ft/ftattacks4combo.c` for `ftCo_800CECE8`, `ftCo_800CED30`, `ftCo_AttackS42_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackS42_IASA`, `ftCo_AttackS42_Phys`, and `ftCo_AttackS42_Coll`.
`ftCo_800CECE8` now checks `fp->cmd_vars[0]` and input bit `fp->input.x668 & 0x100`, calls `ftCo_800CED30`, and returns a boolean result.

`ftCo_800CED30` implements the fighter-kind gating, emits the original `OSReport("don't have smash42 motion!!!\n")` and `__assert("ftattacks4combo.c", 0x36, "0")` failure path, clears `fp->allow_interrupt`, changes to motion state `0x155`, and calls `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.
AttackS42 Anim, Phys, and Coll functions were implemented as simple wrappers around the existing AttackS4 handlers.
`ftCo_AttackS42_IASA` checks bit 7 of `x2218` and calls `ftCo_Wait_IASA` when set.
Updated `src/melee/ft/ftattacks4combo.h` so `ftCo_800CED30` has a typed signature: `void ftCo_800CED30(ftCo_GObj* gobj)` instead of `UNK_RET ftCo_800CED30(UNK_PARAMS)`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` to build `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftattacks4combo.c.o` instead of the old assembly object.

Adjusted `config/GALE01/splits.txt` data and sdata end addresses for the now-C-emitted strings: `.data` end changed from `0x803C6F78` to `0x803C6F72`, and `.sdata` end changed from `0x804D3CD8` to `0x804D3CD2`.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` sizes and string annotations for `lbl_803C6F40`, `lbl_803C6F60`, and `ftCo_804D3CD0`.
smart_moves: Preserved exact assert/report strings and source line number, which matters for matching `.data`/`.sdata` layout and call operands.
Used existing common side-smash routines (`ftCo_AttackS4_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackS4_Phys`, `ftCo_AttackS4_Coll`) instead of duplicating behavior for AttackS42 wrapper states.
Replaced an unknown header prototype with a concrete `void ftCo_800CED30(ftCo_GObj* gobj)` signature, improving type information for future callers.
Captured the redundant branch shape in `ftCo_800CED30` with deliberately structured `goto` code rather than simplifying to a clean switch, prioritizing binary match over source aesthetics.
Updated all build/config surfaces together: source object list, matching status in `configure.py`, removed asm object reference, and corrected split/symbol metadata.
decomp_lessons: For tiny wrapper action-state files, exact match may still require non-idiomatic control flow if the original compiler emitted redundant branches.
When replacing an asm object with C, remember both build systems used here: `configure.py` matching status and `obj_files.mk` object path must be updated.
The object path in `obj_files.mk` must move from `asm/.../*.s.o` to `src/.../*.c.o`; changing only the suffix while leaving `asm` is wrong.
String sizes in `symbols.txt` and section boundaries in `splits.txt` may shrink once strings are emitted by C instead of hand-aligned asm; verify `.data` and `.sdata` ranges after matching.
Wrapper functions can often be matched by forwarding to an existing base action implementation, but IASA bit checks may still need direct field/bit expression matching.

Build failures after exposing new headers may come from downstream init files missing includes; in this PR, a reviewer specifically pointed to missing `#include "ftCommon/ftCo_AttackS4.h"` in `ftCl_Init` and `ftLk_Init`.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action functions use the `ftCo_` prefix.
Side-smash combo state wrappers are named `ftCo_AttackS42_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackS42_IASA`, `ftCo_AttackS42_Phys`, and `ftCo_AttackS42_Coll`.
The matched file keeps the lowercase module name `ftattacks4combo` for source, header, and original assert filename string.
Typed fighter object parameters use `ftCo_GObj*` or `Fighter_GObj*`; this PR's header exposes `ftCo_GObj*` for `ftCo_800CED30`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit nested `if`/`else` plus labels and `goto` in `ftCo_800CED30` to reproduce redundant branch instructions from the original assembly.
Kept `OSReport` before `__assert` in the invalid fighter-kind path to match the original failure sequence.
Used `(u32) fp->cmd_vars[0] != 0` and `fp->input.x668 & 0x100` to match the command-variable/input gate in `ftCo_800CECE8`.
Expressed the IASA interrupt check as `((((Fighter*) gobj->user_data)->x2218 >> 7) & 1)` before calling `ftCo_Wait_IASA`.
Adjusted symbol metadata to mark string objects with exact sizes: `lbl_803C6F40` size `0x1E`, `lbl_803C6F60` size `0x12`, and `ftCo_804D3CD0` size `0x2`.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested changing the removed asm object entry to a C object entry for `ftattacks4combo`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Corrected the path: the replacement object should be `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftattacks4combo.c.o\`, not under `asm`.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Reported a likely build failure from missing `#include "ftCommon/ftCo_AttackS4.h"` in `ftCl_Init` and `ftLk_Init`. The provided changed-file list does not show those files, so the exact fix is not evidenced in the diff excerpt.

review_feedback.author: in-amber
review_feedback.feedback: Commented that the issue should be fixed.

follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that rename or type `ftCo_800CECE8` and `ftCo_800CED30`.
Check whether `ftCl_Init` and `ftLk_Init` received the missing `ftCommon/ftCo_AttackS4.h` include in this PR or a follow-up commit.
Compare other AttackS4/AttackS42 files for reusable wrapper patterns and IASA bit checks.
Look for future cleanup of non-idiomatic `goto` control flow once compiler/matching constraints are better understood.
